subject: Geology
title: Lateral Continuity
description: A quick, practical guide to Lateral Continuity.

hook.text: A river cuts a canyon in half. The layers on both sides still match. They were once a single, continuous sheet.

buildup.text: Lateral continuity means sediment layers extend in all directions until they thin out or hit a barrier.

discovery.text: This allows geologists to match layers on opposite sides of valleys, faults, or even different mountains.

twist.text: It also explains why matching rock layers appear on different continents. They were once connected.

climax.text: Lateral continuity turns isolated outcrops into a connected map. Separate cliffs tell the same story.

punchline.text: Layers that match were once one.

quiz.question: What does lateral continuity mean?
options: Rock layers only exist in one spot
Sediment layers extend continuously until they thin out or hit a barrier
